content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 19: The edit stack

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to creation and editing: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
EDITING & REFINEMENT | STEADY STATE | 18 min

THE BRIEF:
Improve work in passes rather than asking for one magical rewrite.

THE SITUATION:
Bring a draft you care about. Today the model edits it in layers: clarity, structure, tone, and usefulness.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Improve work in passes rather than asking for one magical rewrite. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask for a clarity edit only. Accept or reject the changes.

2. Ask for a structure edit only. No tone polishing yet.

3. Ask for a final tone pass for your actual audience.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- Each pass changes a different thing.
- You reject at least one suggestion instead of accepting the whole output.
- The final version is recognisably yours, just less foggy.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to emails, updates, proposals, posts, reports, scripts, briefs, and all the places where a first draft normally arrives late wearing a suspicious hat.

COACHING NOTE:
Layered editing reduces the "AI voice" problem because each pass has a job. One-pass rewrites tend to over-smooth everything into corporate custard.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Note which edit layer gave the most value. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
